project.title: Multiplexed drug-based selection and counterselection genetic manipulations in Drosophila
project.projectDescription: We describe a drug-based, genetic platform consisting of four selection and two counterselection markers eliminating the need to screen for transgenic progeny. These markers work reliably individually, or in combination, to produce specific genetic outcomes.We perform immunoprecipitation followed by proteomic analysis on one tagged BAC line demonstrating our platform’s applicability to biological discovery.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: For co-immunoprecipitaiton, the fly heads were immunoprecipitated against the GFP antibody. Standard in-gel digestion using trypsin enzyme was done followed by nanoLC-MS/MS analysis
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The MS/MS spectra were searched using the Mascot software. The gene product inference and iBAQ based quanitifiation was carried out using gpGrouper algorithm.

references.referenceLine: Matinyan N, Karkhanis MS, Gonzalez Y, Jain A, Saltzman A, Malovannaya A, Sarrion-Perdigones A, Dierick HA, Venken KJT. Multiplexed drug-based selection and counterselection genetic manipulations in Drosophila. Cell Rep. 2021 36(11):109700

Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Fusion (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA) mass spectrometer.

Comment[Separation]: two-column setup using a pre-column trap of 2cm × 100μm size (Reprosil-Pur Basic C18 1.9 μm, Dr. Maisch GmbH, Germany) and a 20cm × 75μm analytical column (Reprosil-Pur Basic C18 1.9 μm, Dr.Maisch GmbH, Germany)
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: a maximum of two missed cleavages was allowed.
